topic: Flagship upgrade of ordinal principal-strata partial identification: derive an explicit K=3 compatibility-facet certificate and phase threshold for strict nonrectangularity beyond pairwise adjacent-stratum bounds. Pre-anchor check: closest banked result is pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1, accepted only at field tier with an adjacent-chain posterior LP; closest published anchors are Frangakis-Rubin principal stratification and ordinal principal-score / principal-ignorability work. Why non-trivial? The target is not a new-domain LP dual or another Manski interval: require a named facet normal, a minimal K=3 witness law, and a proof that the shared middle stratum creates strict containment of the common-law feasible polytope inside all pairwise relaxations, with an endpoint-attaining certificate. Why promising? The nonroutine object is the hand-derived compatibility facet and strict-containment witness. If the delta reduces to LP duality, support coverage, or definition-unfold frontier algebra, reject or downgrade.

open_problems.open_problem: Identify the K=3 ordinal principal-strata shared-middle compatibility obstruction as an explicit facet normal for the common-law feasible polytope, rather than only as generic nonrectangularity.

source_refs.claim: Defines principal strata by joint potential values of the post-treatment variable, making the common latent law the object that adjacent reductions must be projections of.
source_refs.author: Frangakis and Rubin

source_refs.claim: Principal-score identification treats observed cells as mixtures over latent principal strata under principal ignorability, but does not provide a K>=3 adjacent-posterior-chain facet.
source_refs.author: Ding and Lu
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B

source_refs.excerpt: sharp finite-support adjacent-chain posterior LP with nonrectangular ordinal compatibility gap
shared middle stratum creates compatibility facets that are absent from any product
no single ordinal posterior coordinate q_{z,b}(x,y) can project to both edges

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature supplies the Frangakis-Rubin latent-stratum target and Ding-Lu principal-score mixture formulas, while the banked parent proves sharpness and strict containment at field tier; neither side records a named normal vector, irredundancy proof, or standalone K=3 facet certificate for the shared-middle compatibility obstruction.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to intersect the two adjacent-stratum principal-score relaxations fails because each relaxation can choose its own middle-stratum posterior coordinate, while the K=3 common law must use one shared q_b across both adjacent projections.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A K=3 theorem that fixes coordinates, names the compatibility inequality and facet normal, proves the inequality supports a facet of the common-law feasible polytope, and shows a pairwise-relaxation point separated by that facet.

open_problems.open_problem: Turn the existing strict-gap arithmetic into a minimal K=3 endpoint-attaining witness law for the compatibility facet.

source_refs.claim: Derives explicit sharp bounds for an ordinal relative-treatment-effect functional and proves sharpness by constructing endpoint-attaining probability matrices.
source_refs.author: Lu, Zhang, and Ding

source_refs.claim: Uses principal stratification for truncation by death and large-sample bounds, illustrating that principal-effect sharpness often needs explicit latent-stratum constructions.
source_refs.author: Zhang and Rubin
source_refs.venue: Journal of Educational and Behavioral Statistics

source_refs.excerpt: Add an explicit K=3 finite-support witness showing strict containment
coherent ordinal cell has sup q_b^1=7/15; edgewise relaxation allows r_b^1=5/9

open_problems.why_unsolved: Ordinal sharp-bounds papers show how endpoint-attaining matrices certify sharpness, and the parent review explicitly asked for a K=3 finite-support witness; the later parent note contains numerical Gamma=2 and general-Gamma strictness arithmetic, but not a packaged minimal witness theorem with the active facet, endpoint law, and infeasibility certificate for the pairwise endpoint.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to certify sharpness by giving endpoint optimizers for each adjacent pair fails because those endpoint optimizers can require mutually inconsistent copies of the shared middle stratum once they are embedded in one principal-strata law.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite table of rational atom masses for K=3, the induced P and pi, the coherent endpoint q, the pairwise endpoint r, and a one-line separating-facet evaluation proving the pairwise endpoint cannot be realized by any common law.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive a phase threshold for when the K=3 shared-middle compatibility facet becomes active and forces strict containment beyond all adjacent pairwise relaxations.

source_refs.claim: For ordinal outcomes, treatment-benefit estimands depend on the unobserved joint distribution of potential outcomes and require sharp bounds from marginals.
source_refs.author: Lu, Ding, and Dasgupta
source_refs.venue: Journal of Educational and Behavioral Statistics

source_refs.claim: Provides explicit ordinal sharp-bound formulas, highlighting the value of closed-form thresholds over merely solving an LP numerically.
source_refs.author: Lu, Zhang, and Ding

source_refs.excerpt: for every finite Gamma>1 there is a nonempty relative-open set
binary upper value exceeds the ordinal upper value by (Gamma+2)/(3(Gamma+1)(2Gamma+1))
strict gap is not an isolated equality artifact

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published ordinal-bound literature emphasizes explicit formulas, and the banked parent gives boundary regimes plus a positive-gap example/open-set statement; neither gives a symbolic phase boundary in the observable and principal-score parameters at which the shared-middle compatibility facet first controls the endpoint.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to read the threshold from continuity of the LP value fails because continuity only preserves a known positive gap locally and does not identify the active basis or the parameter inequality where the facet starts binding.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A scalar inequality in Gamma, cell outcome probabilities, and principal-score weights that separates a pairwise-sharp regime from a strict-containment regime, with active-constraint certificates and endpoint constructions on both sides.

open_problems.open_problem: State a fixed-pi compatibility theorem that cleanly separates the ordinal facet mechanism from stronger principal-ignorability, auxiliary-variable, or principal-score-uncertainty assumptions.

source_refs.claim: Principal-score methods rely on principal ignorability, which the authors describe as strong and hard to justify in practice.
source_refs.author: Feller, Mealli, and Miratrix
source_refs.venue: Journal of Educational and Behavioral Statistics

source_refs.claim: Develops identification of principal causal effects using auxiliary variables, showing that extra assumptions are a separate route from pure compatibility geometry.
source_refs.author: Jiang and Ding

source_refs.claim: Studies partial identification of principal causal effects under violations of principal ignorability, reinforcing that weakening PI changes the identified region rather than restoring point identification.
source_refs.author: Wu and Antonelli

source_refs.excerpt: known principal-score vector pi is replaced by a supplied principal-score polytope
pi(x) is known or supplied by external design information

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature offers stronger identification assumptions and recent PI-violation partial-ID results, while the parent assumes fixed or externally supplied pi and only flags a pi-polytope extension; the open gap is to keep the flagship theorem on the fixed-pi ordinal compatibility facet so the mechanism is not confounded with auxiliary identification or principal-score uncertainty.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to invoke principal ignorability or auxiliary-variable identification fails because it changes the feasible set by adding independence or proxy information, whereas the target facet should already follow from one fixed-pi K=3 common-law compatibility constraint.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem stated under randomization, consistency, fixed supplied pi, nonnegativity, and adjacent posterior constraints only, followed by an optional corollary describing whether the same facet survives projection when pi ranges over a supplied polytope.

literature_map:
Frangakis and Rubin (2002, Biometrics) define principal strata as joint potential values of a post-treatment variable and anchor the common-law target.
Ding and Lu (2017, JRSSB) develop principal-score identification under principal ignorability and describe observed cells as latent-strata mixtures.
Jiang, Yang, and Ding (2022, JRSSB) and related principal-score work give point-identified PI baselines, not fixed-pi ordinal compatibility facets.
Feller, Mealli, and Miratrix (2017, JEBS) stress that principal ignorability assumptions are strong in practice, motivating sensitivity/partial-ID geometry.
Nguyen, Stuart, Scharfstein, and Ogburn (2024) and Wu and Antonelli (2024) study PI violations, but not a finite K=3 adjacent posterior-chain facet.
Long and Hudgens (2013, Biometrics), Zhang and Rubin (2003, JEBS), and Imai (2008, SPL) provide principal-effect bounds and sharpness examples mostly in binary/truncation geometries.
Lu, Ding, and Dasgupta (2018, JEBS) and Lu, Zhang, and Ding (2020, Biometrics) are close ordinal sharp-bound anchors, but their transportation problem couples potential-outcome marginals rather than principal-strata posterior chains.
VanderWeele (2011) warns that ordinal or continuous intermediates are often dichotomized and that dichotomization can mislead, matching the pairwise-relaxation failure mode.
Jin and Rubin (2008, JASA) show extended partial compliance can be formulated in principal strata, but do not supply the K=3 facet or phase threshold.
The live gap is not another LP dual or Manski interval: it is the named K=3 facet normal, minimal witness law, and phase threshold for strict containment of the common-law polytope inside all adjacent pairwise relaxations.
Study-pipeline gaps on LATE covariates/defiers and Manski interval inference were inspected as background partial-ID geometry but are not directly relevant to this ordinal principal-strata mechanism.
prior_proposal_map:
Subagent A inspected doc/formalization/** and found the parent accepted-tier artifact under CausalSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/candidates/pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1, not _bank/accepted.
The parent README records ACCEPT at field tier for a sharp finite-support adjacent-chain posterior LP with a nonrectangular ordinal compatibility gap.
The parent proposal's Conjecture 1 names the shared-middle compatibility phenomenon, but leaves the named facet normal and phase threshold as natural upgrade targets.
Reviewer JSON angle0_v5 gives the highest-leverage strengthening path: add an explicit K=3 finite-support strict-containment witness and consider a pi-polytope follow-on.
The parent Stage 0 derivation contains witness arithmetic, including Gamma=2 values and a general gap formula, but it is not packaged as a minimal endpoint-attaining facet certificate.
The closest adjacent repo analogue is pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1, whose shared-middle zig-zag response-path result is useful as a certificate template but has a different latent object and does not solve this principal-strata facet.
The active target folder CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/pid_ordinal_principal_facet_phase contains only run/state initialization files, so there is no duplicate target proposal yet.
